what_it_does: Styles the shared room chrome: a fixed pill-shaped room navigation bar and full-screen room panels that slide in directionally over a gallery, with light paper and dark instrument grounds, plus a mobile bottom-rail adaptation.
observations: The #roombar is a centered, blurred, pill-shaped fixed bar at z-index 70 with .rb icon+label buttons and .on/.hover states.
.room panels are fixed full-screen overlays (z-index 60) toggled via .show and animated with .from-right/.from-left, .moving, and .in classes.
Two data-ground themes: paper (light radial page/warm/cool) and instrument (dark charcoal/bronze radial with remapped ink variables).
The instrument ground adds a faint drafting-grid pseudo-element in the top-right corner using CSS masks.
At <=920px the roombar becomes a dark bottom rail with vertical icon+label buttons and safe-area padding.
All color, spacing, type, radius, shadow, and motion values are pulled from --dna-* custom properties.
standouts: CSS custom properties completely drive color, space, type, and motion — no hardcoded values for core styling.
The instrument ground locally redefines semantic ink variables (--body, --secondary, --faint, --line, --panel) inside a data attribute block.
Direction-aware spatial transitions use arrival classes (.from-left / .from-right) to slide panels onto the canvas.
The mobile layout is explicitly described as matching predicted screen mockups.
conventions: CSS variables for tokens and color-mix for translucent variants.
Fixed-position chrome with explicit z-index layering.
Responsive adaptation via a single max-width media query.
Use of env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for notched devices.
concerns: body{padding-bottom: ...} is set inside a component-specific file, touching global page layout.
.dna-atlas-chip uses !important to clear the rail, suggesting a specificity workaround.
.room.show switches display:none to display:block, which can interrupt transform animations if not sequenced carefully.
No prefers-reduced-motion guard for the sliding panel motion.
Heavy use of color-mix and radial-gradient/mask may have browser support or performance implications on lower-end devices.
notes: This file is the shared shell for a spatial, themed room system: a floating desktop pill nav, directional full-screen panels, and a bottom-rail mobile layout.
questions: Where are the --dna-* variables defined and what is their full token set?
What JavaScript toggles .show, .moving, .in, and the arrival direction classes?
What is .dna-atlas-chip and where is its base styling defined?
How many rooms exist and how are they mapped to modes.json?
Is the instrument drafting grid purely decorative CSS or derived from an asset?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation: it provides the shared room bar and panel chrome, implements the two documented grounds, and routes all visual values through DNA variables.
intention_signals: Create a consistent, theme-aware container system where rooms feel like places on a single canvas, entering from the direction of the user's path, with navigation that adapts from a floating desktop pill to a mobile bottom rail.
novelty: The instrument ground locally re-inks semantic color variables via CSS custom property overrides within a data attribute, paired with a faint drafting-grid pseudo-element to signal a dark studio mode, while using arrival-relative slide transforms for spatial continuity.
contribution: A reusable, token-driven shell for full-screen room overlays and their navigation bar, including two distinct visual grounds and a responsive mobile rail, which a fused system could keep as the shared room chrome.
summary_for_embedding: CSS module that defines a fixed pill-shaped room navigation bar, full-screen sliding room panels with paper and instrument grounds, shared interior typography and panel scaffolding, and a bottom-rail mobile adaptation.
intention_for_embedding: Provide the shared visual and motion chrome for a spatial, direction-aware room system across light document and dark drafting modes.
